title: 3D Bounding Boxes
concept: A 3D bounding box represents an object using 9 parameters: 3D center location, 3D dimensions, and roll, pitch, and yaw rotation.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why is depth calculation crucial for 3D object detection?

options.text: It determines the physical distance of the object from the sensor
It adjusts the brightness of the camera lens
It removes the background color layers

exercises.explain: Correct! Depth is the key variable that translates screen coordinates into real-world physical distances.

cards.title: The Nine Numbers
cards.body: A simple 2D box has only 4 numbers (x, y, width, height). How many numbers does it take to describe a 3D box in space, and what do they represent? Let's count the variables.

cards.title: 3D Box Representation
cards.body: A complete 3D bounding box is described by 9 variables (degrees of freedom): 3D center location (x, y, z), 3D dimensions (width w, height h, length l), and 3D rotation angles (roll, pitch, yaw).

cards.title: Flight Controls
cards.body: Think of an airplane. You need its GPS position (latitude x, longitude y, altitude z), its size (wingspan w, height h, fuselage length l), and its orientation: roll (wings tilting left/right), pitch (nose pointing up/down), and yaw (nose steering left/right).

cards.title: Car 3D Coordinates
cards.body: A self-driving car detects a truck: center = [1.5m left, 12m ahead, 0.5m off-ground], dimensions = [2.2m wide, 3.1m tall, 6.5m long], and yaw = 45 degrees (meaning the truck is turning across the intersection).

cards.title: Always Need 9 Variables?
cards.body: You might think models always predict all 9 variables. Fact: For ground vehicles, roll and pitch are often assumed to be zero because roads are relatively flat. This reduces the prediction target to 7 variables (x,y,z, w,h,l, and yaw), simplifying training.

cards.title: Roll, Pitch, and Yaw
cards.body: How 3D rotation angles are defined in space:

left.text:
Roll: Rotation around the length axis (side tilt)
Pitch: Rotation around the width axis (forward/backward tilt)
Often close to zero on roads

right.text:
Rotation around the vertical height axis
Represents steering direction of a vehicle
Most critical rotation for self-driving

exercises.prompt: How many variables (degrees of freedom) are typically used to represent a complete 3D bounding box in space?

options.text: 9 variables (x, y, z center; w, h, l size; roll, pitch, yaw rotation)
4 variables (x, y coordinates and width, height)

exercises.explain: Correct! A full 3D box requires 3 position coordinates, 3 size dimensions, and 3 rotation angles.

exercises.prompt: True or False: For road-based self-driving cars, roll and pitch are often set to zero, leaving yaw as the primary predicted rotation angle.
exercises.explain: True. Cars stay flat on the road, so yaw (steering direction) is the most critical rotation to predict.

exercises.sentence: The steering direction of a vehicle on a flat road is represented by its ___ rotation angle.
exercises.explain: Yaw represents rotation around the vertical axis, matching steering heading.

exercises.prompt: What do the x, y, and z variables represent in a 3D bounding box?

options.text: The 3D coordinates of the object's center point relative to the sensor
The width, height, and length of the object
The coordinates of the three outer corners

exercises.explain: Correct! [x, y, z] defines the 3D position of the object's center in physical space.

exercises.prompt: You are writing a 3D detector for a warehouse drone. The drone must detect boxes stored on slanted, tilted ramp shelves. Should you simplify the rotation to yaw only?

options.text: No, because the shelves are slanted, the boxes will have active roll and pitch angles that the drone must predict to align its gripper.
Yes, because yaw is the only angle that drones can measure.
Yes, because slanted boxes do not have length values.
No, because drones do not use 3D boxes.

exercises.explain: Exactly! When objects are tilted in multiple directions, predicting roll and pitch is mandatory for precise alignment.
